items.title: Kimi K3 documentation reveals Standard/High/Max reasoning-effort tiers
items.summary: Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 launched July 16 running only at maximum reasoning effort on every call. Updated documentation spotted July 18 maps out three effort tiers - Standard, High, and Max - with the lighter tiers rolling out to the web app, mobile apps, and API after shipping max-only at launch.
items.whyItMatters: Effort-tiered reasoning (cheap/fast for simple grammar or dictionary lookups, max effort reserved for harder tutoring judgments) is exactly the kind of dial worth having on the tutor's DeepSeek/Kimi fallback calls - worth checking pricing on the lower tiers once they actually ship.

items.title: Microsoft preps Project Perception, a multi-model AI security tool undercutting Anthropic's Mythos on cost
items.summary: Microsoft is reportedly readying Project Perception, a vulnerability-scanning-and-patching tool that routes each security task to whichever of Microsoft's, OpenAI's, or Anthropic's models is cheapest and best-suited for it, with a launch targeted as soon as this month. Its central pitch is cost: Anthropic's comparable tool, Mythos, reportedly runs far more expensive per API call than Opus or GPT, and Microsoft says per-task model routing is how it undercuts that.
items.whyItMatters: The same per-task, cheapest-capable-model routing behind Project Perception is the pattern the tutor already leans on - OpenAI Realtime for Romain, ElevenLabs for Anna, DeepSeek for cheap fallback calls - a real-world example of that architecture paying off at production scale.
